Q3 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For an ideal op-amp, which of the following properties is not correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
For ideal op-amp, CMRR is infinite. i.e. differential gain is infinite and common-mode gain is zero

Q4 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For the circuit given below,

If X1 and X2 are inductors and X3 is capacitor, then the circuit is know as

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In Hartley Oscillator, X1 and X2 are inductors and X3 is a capacitor and in the Colpitts oscillator, X1 and X2 are capacitors and X3 is an inductor.

Correct answer: C

(c)

Not attempted

For an input of mV in the circuit shown, the value of maximum frequency that may be used if the op-amp has the slew rate SR = 0.4 V/μs is _______ kHz. (Rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2 (0.28 - 0.34)
Solution

2 (0.28 - 0.34)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the integrator circuit shown in figure,

Assume that voltage VC across the capacitor is zero at t = 0. A step input voltage of V1 = –1V is applied at t = 0. The time constant required such that the output reaches +10 V at t = 1 ms is _______ msec.

NAT
Correct answer: 0.1 (0.08 - 0.12)
Solution

0.1 (0.08 - 0.12)

Then, the resistance R2 is ____________ kΩ.

NAT
Correct answer: 20 (20 - 20)
Solution

20 (20 - 20)

Q11 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

An amplifier with feedback has voltage gain equal to 40. To produce a specified output, the input voltage required without feedback is 0.1 V. When feedback has been provided the input must be increased to 2.4 V to produce the same output. The value of feedback ratio is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q12 Not checked

Not attempted

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q13 Not checked

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
